fields.summary: Guidance needed to create first Jenkins pipeline for Python Flask service
fields.description: We are a new team setting up Jenkins for our Python Flask analytics service. We need to know how to write a Jenkinsfile, connect our GitHub repository, run our pytest test suite, build a Docker image, and deploy to our container registry. We have looked at some examples online but they do not match our company Jenkins setup. We need to know which agent label to use and which credential IDs are available.

content.text: We use Python 3.11 and pytest for testing. Our repo is company/analytics-service. We need to know the agent label for Python projects, the credential IDs for GitHub and our Docker registry, and how to set up the Multibranch Pipeline job.

For Python use agent label python-agent. Credential IDs are the same across all languages: github-credentials for GitHub and registry-credentials for Docker. Steps: pip install, pytest, docker build, docker push. Create a Multibranch Pipeline job in Jenkins pointing to your GitHub repo using github-credentials. This is the third new team onboarding question this month - I am going to create a proper Jenkins onboarding guide in Confluence this week.
Pipeline working now. Thank you. A Confluence guide would have saved us 2 days of back and forth. Looking forward to it.
